issue_title: Inconsistent computation of gradient in MaxUnPooling
surface_reasoning: Priority 1: carries `module: correctness (silent)` label — PyTorch's explicit marker for silent wrong-answer bugs. labeled `actionable`. Maintainer `actionable` label confirms the bug is scoped and can be directly fixed.

linked_prs.title: Add nondeterministic alert for `MaxUnpool1d/2d/3d`
linked_prs.abandoned_reason: Likely a ghstack artifact. COLLABORATOR lezcano force-merged with '@pytorchbot merge -f flaky tests' and github-actions subsequently posted 'You've committed this PR', indicating the code landed. However, the GitHub API reports merged=false and the merge_commit_sha is not resolvable on main. This PR added the nondeterminism alert/warning for MaxUnpool, which is a partial fix. The underlying backward gradient bug (all inputs receiving gradient instead of only the correct one) was not addressed by this PR.
linked_prs.reviews_summary: COLLABORATOR lezcano APPROVED on 2022-09-10. COLLABORATOR mruberry APPROVED on 2022-09-15. COLLABORATOR nikitaved APPROVED on 2022-09-16. COLLABORATOR kurtamohler triggered @pytorchbot merge twice (both failed due to CI). COLLABORATOR lezcano then triggered @pytorchbot merge -f 'flaky tests' (force merge). github-actions bot commented 'You've committed this PR' confirming the code landed despite API showing merged=false.

linked_prs.title: add reproducibility notes to nn.UnpoolND operations
linked_prs.abandoned_reason: Documentation fix PR. COLLABORATOR albanD APPROVED. CONTRIBUTOR drisspg triggered @pytorchbot merge and pytorchmergebot confirmed 'Merge started'. GitHub API shows merged=false — consistent with ghstack behavior. Addresses documentation for the nondeterminism, not the backward correctness bug.
linked_prs.reviews_summary: COLLABORATOR albanD APPROVED. CONTRIBUTOR drisspg triggered @pytorchbot merge and pytorchmergebot confirmed 'Merge started'. GitHub API reports merged=false, but the 'Merge started' confirmation suggests the code likely landed.

linked_prs.title: [functorch] reclassifying max_unpool failures
linked_prs.abandoned_reason: ghstack artifact. The PR body starts with 'Stack from [ghstack]:' and lists 5 PRs in the stack. Reclassified functorch test failures for max_unpool as expected given non-determinism. No reviews from COLLABORATOR/MEMBER/OWNER. Not directly a correctness fix.
linked_prs.reviews_summary: No COLLABORATOR/MEMBER/OWNER reviews. CONTRIBUTOR zou3519 commented. This was part of a functorch ghstack stack; closed when the stack was processed.

maintainer_signals.author: lezcano
maintainer_signals.quote: Could you check whether this function passes `gradcheck` with your inputs of choice?

maintainer_signals.author: nikitaved
maintainer_signals.quote: gradcheck will fail with repeated indices, I am afraid, that is why OpInfo tests make sure that there are no repeated indices. I think the backward kernel just does not take into consideration any repeated indices at all.

maintainer_signals.author: lezcano
maintainer_signals.quote: So, the underlying issue here is that `max` is not differentiable when the maximum value is not unique. I think what we should do here is to document this the `MaxPool*d` functions.

maintainer_signals.author: nikitaved
maintainer_signals.quote: Having a single value getting all the grad is a valid subgradient, actually. This behavior could get implemented if forward is aware of repeated indices. In the example it is all the values that get grads, which, I would assume, is actually wrong and is a legit bug.

maintainer_signals.author: albanD
maintainer_signals.quote: In any case, this should be marked as non-deterministic properly.

confidence_justification: Start: 0.50. Step 1: 'module: correctness (silent)' label present → +0.25. Running total: 0.75. Step 2: repro script present — complete Python code block demonstrating the incorrect gradient propagation (all 3 inputs sharing index 20 receive gradient value 20 instead of only the last one) → +0.15. Running total: 0.90. Step 3: 'triaged' label present → +0.10. Running total: 1.00. Step 4: issue open 2+ years (2022 to 2026) with no full resolution → +0.05. Running total: 1.05. Step 5: COLLABORATOR approved_fix signal — COLLABORATOR lezcano force-merged PR #84766 (nondeterminism alert, partial fix), COLLABORATORs lezcano/mruberry/nikitaved all approved that PR → +0.20. Running total: 1.25. Step 6: COLLABORATOR requested_merge — kurtamohler (COLLABORATOR) triggered @pytorchbot merge twice; lezcano (COLLABORATOR) triggered @pytorchbot merge -f → +0.20. Running total: 1.45. Step 7: no blocking signals. No hard blockers. still_reproducible = 'likely': Jan 2025 comment from user shaoyuyoung confirms 'this issue still exists on CPU'. No negative adjustments apply. fix_scope = single_module (changes required in MaxUnpooling.cpp and potentially MaxUnpooling.cu): does not qualify for the +0.10 (only single_function or single_file qualify). Clamp to [0.0, 1.0]. Final: 1.00.
ground_truth_delta: This finding very closely matches the positive_71774 profile: silent correctness bug, correctness (silent) + triaged + actionable labels, repro script present, multiple COLLABORATOR-approved PRs that did not fully merge, issue open 3+ years. Key differences from 71774: (1) the fix scope is broader — CPU backward kernel in MaxUnpooling.cpp requires logic to only propagate gradient to the last occurrence of a repeated index, analogous to how max/min handle non-unique extrema; (2) three separate COLLABORATORs approved fix PRs (stronger signal than 71774's single COLLABORATOR), and a force-merge was attempted; (3) the core backward bug is partially addressed (nondeterminism alert added, CUDA may have been fixed) but CPU backward is confirmed still broken as of Jan 2025, so the finding is still fully actionable; (4) the 'actionable' label is still on the issue as of the fetch date, providing an explicit maintainer signal of actionability beyond what 71774 had.

contribution_path.title: Read the abandoned PR diff
contribution_path.description: Study PR #84766 ('Add nondeterministic alert for `MaxUnpool1d/2d/3d`') to understand the approach before writing any code. Note which files changed and the overall diff size. The PR received a COLLABORATOR APPROVED review — the fix approach is already validated.
contribution_path.warning: What killed this PR: Likely a ghstack artifact. COLLABORATOR lezcano force-merged with '@pytorchbot merge -f flaky tests' and github-actions subsequently posted 'You've committed this PR', indicating the code landed. Howe

contribution_path.title: Reproduce the bug on current main
contribution_path.description: Clone pytorch/pytorch main and run the minimal repro from issue #80827. Confirm the bug still exists before investing time in the fix.

contribution_path.title: Apply the fix on current main
contribution_path.description: PR #84766 was force-merged with '@pytorchbot merge -f flaky tests' by lezcano and approved by lezcano, mruberry, and albanD — but shows merged=false due to a ghstack artifact. Reopen with the same diff. Additionally, albanD asked to mark the op as non-deterministic via `torch.use_deterministic_algorithms` — include that in the new PR.
contribution_path.warning: ghstack artifact: PR #84766 is closed with merged=false despite force-merge. Do NOT cherry-pick — apply the diff manually.

contribution_path.title: Run tests and add a regression test
contribution_path.description: Run `python test/test_nn.py -k max_unpool`. albanD asked to mark max_unpool as non-deterministic — check `torch/_torch_docs.py` and the op's determinism annotation.

contribution_path.title: Open a new PR
contribution_path.description: Submit a PR with 'Fixes #80827' and 'Revives #84766 and #94629' in the body. Tag @lezcano @nikitaved @albanD for review — they already know the issue and the fix approach.
